title: perityle ciliata
intro: Perityle ciliata, also known as the hairy perityle, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, where it grows in dry regions. Perityle ciliata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, and it has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to take advantage of its health benefits, including wound healing and immune system support.
